notes: All 9 responses correctly nail the chart type, axis labels/ranges, and the three series colors and overall trends — a strong, consistent baseline. The recurring, systematic error across the whole batch is over-reading the two CO2 series' 2018 endpoints (atmospheric ~418-421 ppm vs ~415 plotted; ocean pCO2 ~400-410 ppm vs ~380 plotted), suggesting the model misreads the right-side scale or extrapolates. None of the nine responses mentioned the inset Hawaiian Islands map (a key visual element), and only the three t0.4-rep0/t0.7-rep2 variants named the Mauna Loa/ALOHA stations. The t0.7 reps uniquely fabricated a 'post-1993 inflection/acceleration' that is absent from the roughly linear plotted data — a mild hallucination that drags them below the t0.1/t0.4 cluster.
